name: Asif Ali Zardari
title: President of Pakistan

compatibilityOverrides.reason: CPEC economic dependency and strategic partnership with China
Kashmir dispute and deep historical Indo-Pakistani rivalry
Complex border tensions despite historical ISI connections

biography: Asif Ali Zardari, widower of assassinated former Prime Minister Benazir Bhutto, returned to Pakistan's presidency after years of political exile and imprisonment. Known as 'Mr. Ten Percent' for alleged kickback schemes, his ESTP pragmatism enables survival across Pakistan's treacherous civil-military power dynamics. He balances the powerful military establishment, manages CPEC obligations to China, navigates the nuclear rivalry with India over Kashmir, and contends with internal extremism—all while his PPP party struggles to maintain relevance against populist challengers.
